source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
We make a sequence of figures by dividing a square. The first four figures have 1, 4, 7 and 10 parts, respectively.
<image1>
How many parts will the fifth figure have?

image_dependency: Question explicitly refers to 'a sequence of figures' formed by dividing a square
asset_registry_record.image_manifest shows 1 associated image and registry_passed=true
multi_step: Solving likely involves identifying the visual subdivision pattern across successive figures and then extending it to the fifth figure
The text also provides counts 1, 4, 7, 10, which support a simple progression-based extrapolation
Because the numeric pattern is explicit in the text, the reasoning burden appears moderate rather than high
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text='13' is unambiguous
A proposed solution can be checked directly against the observed count pattern in the first four figures

decision_reasons: asset registry passed with no reported issues
core assets are complete: question text, image, and numeric answer are all present
verifiability is high because the answer is an unambiguous number
the problem has meaningful visual content and associated image support
although there is some ambiguity risk in pattern continuation, the sample still retains sufficient core semantics and is well-supported overall
